title: Cookies Policy
content:
Last updated:
February 5th, 2025

What are cookies exactly?

Cookies are small text files that our website stores on your computer or mobile device when you visit follow.art. These files contain information that can be read by our website during subsequent visits. Each website can only access its own cookies, though some pages may contain elements (and therefore cookies) from multiple sources.

Why do we use cookies?

At follow.art, we use cookies to:

  • Provide you with a consistent and personalized browsing experience.
  • Remember your preferences and settings.
  • Keep you authenticated when you log into your account.
  • Analyze how visitors use our website to improve our services.
  • Enable essential features like shopping carts and wishlists.

What types of cookies do we use?

Analytics Cookies

We use Google Analytics cookies to understand how visitors interact with our website. These anonymous cookies help us:

  • Track unique visitor counts.
  • Identify the most popular pages.
  • Understand user behavior patterns.
  • Gather geographical data about our visitors.
  • Make data-driven decisions to improve our service.

Authentication Cookies

When you log into your follow.art account, we create authentication cookies to:

  • Keep you signed in.
  • Protect your account security.
  • Verify your access permissions.
  • Manage your session state.

These cookies typically expire when you close your browser or shut down your device.

Marketing Cookies

We use marketing cookies to:

  • Track the effectiveness of our advertising campaigns.
  • Enable retargeting through platforms like Google and Facebook.
  • Control ad displays for visitors who have been to our site.
  • Optimize our marketing spend.

No personal information is shared with advertisers through these cookies.

Site Display Cookies

These cookies remember your preferences for:

  • Page display settings.
  • Interface customizations.
  • Dismissed notifications.
  • Accessibility options.

Shopping Cart & Wishlist Cookies

We use these cookies to:

  • Maintain your shopping cart contents.
  • Save items to your wishlist.
  • Provide a seamless shopping experience.
  • Enable guest checkout functionality.

How do I turn cookies off?

While cookies help enhance your experience on follow.art, you can disable them through your browser settings. Here's how to manage cookies in popular browsers:

Firefox

  1. Open the Tools menu.
  2. Select Options.
  3. Click Privacy.
  4. Adjust cookie settings as desired.

For detailed instructions, visit Firefox Cookie Settings Help

Chrome

  1. Click the menu icon (three dots).
  2. Select Settings.
  3. Click "Privacy and security."
  4. Choose "Cookies and other site data."

For detailed instructions, visit Chrome Cookie Settings Help.

Safari

  1. Open Safari Preferences.
  2. Click the Privacy tab.
  3. Adjust cookie settings as needed.

For detailed instructions, visit Safari Cookie Settings Help.

Internet Explorer

  1. Open the Tools menu.
  2. Select Internet Options.
  3. Click the Privacy tab.
  4. Adjust cookie settings as desired.

For detailed instructions, visit Internet Explorer Cookie Settings Help.

Please Note: Disabling cookies may limit your ability to use certain features of follow.art, including:

  • Account login
  • Shopping cart functionality
  • Personalized experiences
  • Saved preferences
  • Access to restricted content

Questions?

If you have any questions about our use of cookies or this policy, please contact us at help@follow.art
